page: Microsoft Azure EC8eds_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the ECEDV6 family. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU.

This server includes 472 GB of local storage and is designated as Premium Storage capable. It does not include any bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The combination of dedicated Intel vCPUs and a high memory allocation positions this instance for workloads requiring substantial memory capacity relative to compute resources.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the EC8eds_v6 offers a balanced resource density for memory-intensive applications without the overhead of GPU hardware. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance. This hardware profile is suited for memory-optimized workloads, database deployments, and enterprise applications that benefit from local storage and high memory-to-vCPU ratios.
description: The Microsoft Azure EC8eds_v6 is a memory-optimized server instance from the ECEDV6 family, built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an Intel processor. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it features 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory, establishing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The instance is equipped with 472 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, though it lacks integrated GPUs and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This configuration delivers a high-density memory profile relative to compute resources, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that do not require accelerator hardware. The EC8eds_v6 is suited for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, and enterprise workloads requiring dedicated CPU performance and local storage capacity.
